sessions.notes: Keep exchanges formulaic; prioritize clear pronunciation and turn-taking.
sessions.title: Greetings and Polite Words
sessions.objective: Use basic greeting and politeness words in short, memorized exchanges.
sessions.context: Meeting people for the first time in class or in public.

sessions.notes: Recycle 'this/that' and 'a/an' in teacher talk.
sessions.title: Classroom Objects
sessions.objective: Identify common objects; respond to 'What is this?' and simple imperatives.
sessions.context: Understanding and following simple classroom instructions.

sessions.notes: Add quick recognition tasks (hearing → pointing/choosing).
sessions.title: Numbers 1–12
sessions.objective: Recognize and say numbers 1–12; use them in short answers.
sessions.context: Counting items and simple personal data (age, phone digits).

sessions.notes: Avoid overload: keep to high-frequency classroom/real-life objects.
sessions.title: Colors
sessions.objective: Name common colors; answer 'What color is it?' with single-word or 2-word responses.
sessions.context: Describing objects and basic preferences.

sessions.notes: Personalize with learner photos or stick figures; keep language chunked.
sessions.title: Family Words
sessions.objective: Identify immediate family members; understand simple 'This is my…' statements.
sessions.context: Talking about close family using simple labels.

sessions.notes: Support with visuals/realia; recycle please/thanks.
sessions.title: Food and Drink Basics
sessions.objective: Name common food/drink items; choose and request items with single words or short chunks.
sessions.context: Very basic needs at home or a café.

sessions.notes: Limit days to a few anchors; expand in A1/A2.
sessions.title: Days and Time Basics
sessions.objective: Use key time words for simple statements and short answers.
sessions.context: Simple scheduling talk and classroom routines.

sessions.notes: Use maps/pictures; keep grammar minimal.
sessions.title: Places in Town
sessions.objective: Recognize common place words; respond to 'Where?' with one-word answers.
sessions.context: Survival navigation and asking where to go.

sessions.notes: Prioritize comprehension; production can be choral/controlled.
sessions.title: Everyday Action Verbs
sessions.objective: Comprehend and perform high-frequency verbs; produce simple verb + object chunks.
sessions.context: Understanding common classroom and daily-life actions.

sessions.notes: Keep distinctions concrete (states) rather than abstract emotions.
sessions.title: Basic Feelings
sessions.objective: Label basic feelings and states; answer 'How are you?' with simple choices.
sessions.context: Checking in and expressing basic comfort/needs.

sessions.notes: Challenge: Combine with 'today/tomorrow' and clothing choices. Keep structures simple but push for fuller responses.
sessions.title: Weather and Seasons (Optional Harder)
sessions.objective: Use weather vocabulary in simple present statements; understand seasonal references.
sessions.context: Describing basic weather conditions and seasonal concepts.

sessions.notes: Challenge: Requires gesturing and more complex pain descriptions. Practice with role-play scenarios.
sessions.title: Body Parts and Health (Optional Harder)
sessions.objective: Identify body parts; express simple pain or illness with 'My ___ hurts' or 'I am sick.'
sessions.context: Expressing basic health needs and body awareness.

sessions.notes: Challenge: Abstract concepts like 'wait' and 'ticket' require more context. Use visual aids extensively.
sessions.title: Transportation and Movement (Optional Harder)
sessions.objective: Name common vehicles; express simple intentions with 'I go by ___' or 'I need a ___.'
sessions.context: Discussing modes of transportation and basic travel.

sessions.notes: Keep to memorized chunks; delay third-person expansion if needed.
sessions.title: Subject Pronouns + BE (affirmative)
sessions.structure: I am / You are / He is / She is
sessions.objective: Produce short identity sentences (My name is…, I am…).
sessions.context: Introductions and identity statements.

sessions.notes: Use real objects; emphasize singular only.
sessions.title: This/That + Noun
sessions.structure: This is a… / That is a…
sessions.objective: Ask/answer 'What is this/that?' with controlled patterns.
sessions.context: Pointing to objects in class and at home.

sessions.notes: Teach as sound-based; avoid exceptions overload.
sessions.title: A / An (indefinite articles)
sessions.structure: a + consonant sound / an + vowel sound
sessions.objective: Use a/an accurately in simple noun phrases.
sessions.context: Naming objects (a pen, an apple).

sessions.notes: Keep irregular plurals for A1 (children, people).
sessions.title: Plural -s + Numbers
sessions.objective: Form regular plurals; match number + noun in short phrases.
sessions.context: Counting classroom items.

sessions.notes: Use substitution drills: my book / your book.
sessions.title: Possessive Adjectives
sessions.objective: Say who something belongs to using short patterns.
sessions.context: Talking about personal items and family.

sessions.notes: Keep meaning concrete; avoid perfect aspect.
sessions.title: HAVE (simple possession)
sessions.objective: State simple possession; answer yes/no questions with have.
sessions.context: Personal items and needs.

sessions.notes: Pair with TPR; add 'please' for politeness.
sessions.title: Imperatives (classroom language)
sessions.structure: Open…, Close…, Sit down…, Please + verb
sessions.objective: Understand and use common imperatives politely.
sessions.context: Classroom management and daily routines.

sessions.notes: Focus on rhythm: Are you…? Yes, I am. / No, I’m not.
sessions.title: BE Questions (Yes/No)
sessions.structure: Are you…? / Is he…? + short answers
sessions.objective: Ask and answer basic yes/no questions with BE.
sessions.context: Simple check-in questions.

sessions.notes: Keep to present only; use visuals.
sessions.title: There is / There are (basic)
sessions.objective: Make simple existence statements about places.
sessions.context: Describing a room with objects.

sessions.notes: Keep verb list limited and high-frequency.
sessions.title: CAN for ability/request (basic)
sessions.objective: Use can to talk about basic ability and ask permission.
sessions.context: Simple abilities and polite permission.

sessions.notes: Challenge: Plural forms and distance concepts are cognitively demanding. Use physical distance demonstrations.
sessions.title: Demonstratives (this/that) + Plural Forms (Optional Harder)
sessions.structure: This is / That is / These are / Those are
sessions.objective: Use demonstratives correctly with singular and plural nouns in controlled situations.
sessions.context: Distinguishing near/far and singular/plural objects.

sessions.notes: Challenge: Requires understanding of third-person possessives which are abstract. Practice with family photos.
sessions.title: Possessive Adjectives with Family (Optional Harder)
sessions.structure: my, your, his, her + family words
sessions.objective: Produce short sentences like 'This is my father' and 'Her name is ___.'
sessions.context: Describing family relationships with possessive markers.

sessions.notes: Challenge: Modal concept is advanced. Focus on memorized chunks before expanding.
sessions.title: Present Simple: CAN for Ability (Optional Harder)
sessions.objective: State simple abilities using 'can/can't' with common verbs (swim, read, speak).
sessions.context: Expressing basic abilities and limitations.

sessions.notes: Use name cards; keep turn-taking highly scaffolded.
sessions.title: Introducing Yourself
sessions.objective: First meeting in class; saying name and country.

sessions.notes: Teach supportive gestures; encourage polite tone.
sessions.title: Classroom Requests
sessions.objective: Asking for help or repeating in class.

sessions.notes: Use real/fake money and price tags; focus on clarity.
sessions.title: Numbers and Prices
sessions.objective: Buying one item and understanding a simple price.

sessions.notes: Use simple maps; limit directions to here/there/left/right.
sessions.title: Where Is…?
sessions.objective: Finding a place in a building (toilet, office).

sessions.notes: Keep menu very small; recycle please/thanks.
sessions.title: Ordering a Drink
sessions.objective: Ordering a tea/coffee at a café counter.

sessions.notes: Allow learners to choose how much personal detail to share.
sessions.title: Talking About Family (Basic)
sessions.objective: Showing a photo and naming family members.

sessions.notes: Use visuals to reduce cognitive load; short turns.
sessions.title: Likes and Dislikes (Basic)
sessions.objective: Saying what you like (food/hobby) in simple form.

sessions.notes: Offer choice cards (fine/tired/happy) for fast participation.
sessions.title: How Are You? (Basic Check-in)
sessions.objective: Short greetings and feeling check at start of class.

sessions.notes: Challenge: Requires confidence to initiate. Provide sentence starters and practice in safe scenarios first.
sessions.title: Making Simple Requests (Optional Harder)
sessions.objective: Asking for help with objects or actions in classroom/public.
sessions.functions: requesting; asking for help; expressing needs

sessions.notes: Challenge: Question formation and short answers are complex. Drill patterns heavily before free use.
sessions.title: Expressing Likes and Dislikes (Optional Harder)
sessions.objective: Talking about personal preferences with food, colors, activities.

sessions.notes: Challenge: Spatial prepositions are abstract. Use physical manipulation and gestures extensively.
sessions.title: Describing Location (Optional Harder)
sessions.objective: Saying where objects or people are using basic prepositions.
